Mount Everest Reaching an elevation of 29,032 feet (8,849 meters), Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world. # Mount Everest. Mount Everest is on the crest of the Great Himalayas of southern Asia. Its most common Tibetan name, Chomolungma, means “Goddess Mother of the World” or “Goddess of the Valley.” The Sanskrit name Sagarmatha means literally “Peak of Heaven.” Mount Everest was also previously referred to as Peak XV; it was renamed for Sir George Everest in 1865. Reaching an elevation of 29,032 feet (8,849 meters), Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.

The result of this was what the Chinese called a “rock height” of 29,017.12 feet (8,844.43 meters), which, though widely reported in the media, was recognized only by China for the next several years.

title: Mount Everest - Kids | Britannica Kids | Homework Help
content: # Mount Everest. Mount EverestSnow and ice surround the peak of Mount Everest, the highest point on Earth.The highest point on Earth is Mount Everest. Everest is one of the Himalayas of southern Asia. The peak is on the border between Nepal and the Chinese region of Tibet. In Tibetan the peak is known as Chomolungma, or Qomolangma, meaning “Goddess Mother of the World.”. Snow and ice surround the peak of Mount Everest, the highest point on Earth. Mount Everest rises to a height of more than 29,000 feet (8,840 meters). The best known are the Sherpa. They live in villages at altitudes up to about 14,000 feet (4,300 meters). The Sherpa are known for their strength and endurance at high altitudes. Mount Everest has long been a challenge to mountaineers. They failed largely because of the harsh conditions on the mountain. Edmund Hillary of New Zealand and Tenzing Norgay, a Nepalese Sherpa guide, were the first to reach the top.

title: Is Mount Everest Really the Tallest Mountain in the World? | Britannica
content: Is Mount Everest Really the Tallest Mountain in the World? Snow-covered peak of Mount Everest rises above rugged, dark mountains and clouds under a clear blue sky in the Himalayas. Mount Everest Snow and ice surround the peak of Mount Everest, the highest point on Earth. Mount Everest is best described as the *highest* mountain in the world. In other words: it has the greatest elevation, at more than 29,000 feet (about 8,850 meters) above sea level. Other mountains, though, have made claims on being the tallest when measured from points other than sea level. Chimborazo in Ecuador, for example, has been cited as the tallest peak when measured from the center of Earth. But Chimborazo is located near the Equator, where Earth bulges, which means that it is about 6,800 feet (about 2,070 meters) farther above Earth’s center than Everest. Measured from base to peak, Hawaii’s Mauna Kea, which originates at the bottom of the ocean, is far taller than Everest, at about 33,500 feet (10,210 meters).
